title: EMUtils
description: A lightweight client-side utilities mod for Minecraft on Fabric.
body:
![EMUtils](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/pf8Myc7A/images/9a883e83dc5de49e30ebb3062c2490bf543b0957.png)

Tired of installing five different mods just to copy a chat message, zoom in, and reconnect after a kick?
EMUtils bundles the small stuff into one lightweight client-side mod for Minecraft on Fabric — so you can stop juggling mods and get back to playing.

## Status EMUtils is in active development. Has a pile of quality-of-life features, and zero interest in getting in your way while you play. For a full list of features, view the [GitHub](https://github.com/palethea/EMUtils). To report a bug, or request a feature visit [EMUtils Feedback](https://palethea.com/emutils/). ## Minecraft Versions EMUtils supports Minecraft 1.21.9-1.21.11 and Minecraft 26.x. - **Minecraft 26.x** is the active development version and receives new features and fixes. - **Minecraft 1.21.9-1.21.11** enters maintenance mode after EMUtils 0.13.0. It will continue receiving bug fixes and compatibility fixes, but new features will target Minecraft 26.x. ## Requirements - Minecraft **1.21.9-1.21.11** or **26.x** - Java 21 or newer for the 1.21.x jar - Java 25 or newer for the 26.x jar - Fabric Loader 0.19.2 or newer for 1.21.x, or 0.19.3 or newer for 26.x - Fabric API ### Optional integrations - **Iris** — required for Pack Manager shader apply/disable actions - **Minescript** — required for Script Manager browsing, editing, running, and EMUtils script keybinds ## Installation 1. Install [Fabric Loader](https://fabricmc.net/use/) for the supported versions. 2. Download the EMUtils jar matching your Minecraft version. 3. Place the jar in your `mods` folder. 4. Launch the game. ## License EMUtils is licensed under the [Apache License 2.0](LICENSE). Copyright 2026 Palethea. If you use, fork, modify, or redistribute EMUtils, keep the original license and attribution notices. ## AI Notice This mod was made with heavy help from Artificial Intelligence. I was simply just tired of having a million mods to do specific things, and wanted an all-in-one mod.
